sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Graphcore brings new competition to Nvidia in latest MLPerf AI benchmarks
feed.summary: Graphcore, a UK-based startup, has demonstrated competitive performance against industry giants Nvidia and Google in the latest MLPerf AI benchmarks. While not securing the top spot in any test, Graphcore's results are significant when considering the fewer chips used and its commercial availability, positioning it as a viable alternative in the AI training market. Despite Nvidia's claims of "cherry-picking" comparisons and maintaining a strong overall lead, Graphcore emphasizes the economic advantage and scalability of its IPU systems.

feed.title: AMD-Xilinx Merger Details Hint at Stronger Chip Supply Moving Forward
feed.summary: The Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) acquisition of Xilinx has been approved by U.K. regulators, paving the way for strengthened global chip supply. The all-stock deal, valued at $35 billion, will see Xilinx shareholders receive 1.7234 AMD shares for each Xilinx share they own, giving AMD shareholders a 74% stake in the merged entity. This merger is expected to significantly ramp up production, helping to alleviate the ongoing global chip shortage.

feed.title: Texas Instruments to buy semiconductor factory in $900 million deal
feed.summary: Texas Instruments announced it will acquire Micron Technology Inc.'s semiconductor factory in Lehi, Utah, for $900 million in cash. This acquisition will be TI's fourth 300-millimeter wafer facility, strengthening its manufacturing capabilities and supply chain control amid a global chip shortage. The company expects the plant to begin generating revenue in the first quarter of 2023, following an investment of approximately $75 million per quarter in 2022.

feed.title: Cadence Report Reveals Consumers’ Perception of Hyperconnectivity
feed.summary: A new report from Cadence Design Systems Inc. indicates that a majority of consumers anticipate hyperconnectivity driven by hyperscale computing will positively affect their lives within five years. The report, "Hyperconnectivity & You – A Roadmap for the Consumer Experience," highlights key themes of confidence, convenience, and collaboration, underscoring the importance of data transparency, device security, and seamless integration of technology in consumer electronics, automotive, and healthcare sectors. Despite relatively low awareness of hyperscale computing, consumers expect significant positive impacts, particularly on mobile phones.

feed.title: HP Unveils Pavilion Aero - its Lightest Consumer Laptop, Powered by AMD
feed.summary: HP Inc. announced its new Pavilion Aero 13 Laptop PC, touted as its lightest consumer laptop at less than 1 kilogram, featuring an AMD Ryzen 7 5800U Mobile Processor. The laptop emphasizes sustainability with recycled materials and a premium design. HP also expanded its M-Series monitors with the M24fwa and M27fwa FHD monitors, which are Eyesafe certified and made with recycled materials.

feed.title: AMD moves closer to acquiring Xilinx
feed.summary: AMD is nearing its acquisition of Xilinx, a merger expected to broaden AMD's market reach into sectors like 5G, automotive, and defense. The article, written by Mike Cronin for the Austin Business Journal, covers the details of this impending deal and also touches on the current chip shortage. This acquisition is anticipated to bring significant profits to AMD.

feed.title: Qualcomm's new CEO eyes dominance in laptop markets
feed.summary: Qualcomm's new CEO, Cristiano Amon, aims to dominate the laptop chip market by next year, challenging Apple's energy-efficient chips with custom-designed processors. The company plans to leverage its acquisition of Nuvia and a team of ex-Apple chip architects to create leading performance for battery-powered devices. Amon also anticipates significant revenue growth from China, despite political tensions, and is open to licensing Nuvia's designs for cloud computing data centers.

feed.title: Focus: Qualcomm's new CEO eyes dominance in the laptop markets
feed.summary: Qualcomm's new CEO, Cristiano Amon, plans to challenge Apple's dominance in the laptop market by developing energy-efficient chips with the help of former Apple chip architects. The company is also focusing on revenue growth from China for its smartphone chip business despite political tensions and aims to leverage its Nuvia acquisition to push 5G connectivity into laptops, providing custom-designed chips to compete with Apple. Amon also faces the challenge of potentially losing Apple as a modem chip customer and improving Qualcomm's brand recognition among consumers.

feed.title: TSMC Wins Intel Order For Two 3nm CPUs To Regain Market Share Lost To AMD
feed.summary: TSMC has secured an order from Intel to produce two 3nm CPUs, signaling Intel's strategy to regain market share lost to AMD, particularly in notebooks and data centers. This move marks a significant shift for Intel, which has historically relied on its own manufacturing facilities, and indicates a larger 3nm chip order for Intel than for Apple. Mass production for TSMC's 3nm process is expected to begin next year, with commercial output of Intel's chips following in 2023.

feed.title: AMD directors dodge shareholder derivative suit over board diversity
feed.summary: A federal judge in California dismissed a shareholder lawsuit against Advanced Micro Devices Inc.'s board, which alleged breach of duty for not including a Black director. The judge ruled that the lawsuit failed to provide specific allegations as to why it would have been futile to demand action directly from the board before suing. This dismissal is one of several similar cases filed since last summer concerning board diversity, with most plaintiffs receiving permission to refile.

feed.title: Chip Shortage Pushes China Auto Sales Down 12% in June
feed.summary: China's auto sales dropped by 12.4% in June compared to the previous year, primarily due to the global semiconductor shortage impacting auto production. Despite the decline, overall sales for the first half of the year were up 25.6%, and new energy vehicle (NEV) sales saw significant growth, jumping 139%. Automakers like Nio, Xpeng, and BYD are expanding NEV manufacturing as the government promotes greener vehicles.

feed.title: Memory chip supercycle cools as demand declines
feed.summary: The global memory chip supercycle is cooling down as DRAM chip price growth weakens, affecting companies like Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. This slowdown is attributed to lower-than-expected server DRAM orders, declining demand for PCs and smartphones, and integrated circuit supply shortages affecting data center expansion. Analysts anticipate the slowdown to persist until the end of the year, though a long-term boom is still expected with server demand recovery next year.

feed.title: Intel exploring deal to buy US chipmaker GlobalFoundries for $30B
feed.summary: Intel Corp. is reportedly in talks to acquire chipmaker GlobalFoundries Inc. for up to $30 billion, which would be Intel's largest acquisition to date. The potential deal aligns with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger's strategy to bolster the company's chip production capabilities and compete with industry leaders like TSMC. Analysts note the acquisition could offer immediate benefits to Intel by providing access to more fabs, while GlobalFoundries' owners would secure a significant payday without the uncertainty of an IPO.

feed.title: TSMC Expects Auto-Chip Shortage to Abate This Quarter
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), the world's largest contract chip maker, anticipates the automotive chip shortage will begin to ease in the coming months due to increased production. The company is on track to boost microcontroller output for cars by about 60% this year. However, TSMC's CEO noted that the broader semiconductor shortage might continue into 2022.

feed.title: Intel is in talks to buy GlobalFoundries for about $30 billion
feed.summary: Intel is reportedly in discussions to acquire GlobalFoundries for approximately $30 billion, a move that would significantly boost Intel's strategy to become a major contract chip manufacturer. This potential acquisition aligns with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger's push to expand chip-making capabilities for other companies, a market currently dominated by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. The deal, if it materializes, would be Intel's largest acquisition ever, although GlobalFoundries is also considering an initial public offering.

feed.title: Intel in talks to buy GlobalFoundries for about $30 billion - WSJ
feed.summary: Intel Corp is reportedly in talks to acquire semiconductor manufacturer GlobalFoundries Inc for approximately $30 billion, according to the Wall Street Journal. This potential deal comes amid a global semiconductor shortage and could help Intel significantly ramp up chip production, although GlobalFoundries has denied direct discussions with Intel. The acquisition could also raise antitrust questions given GlobalFoundries' history with AMD.

feed.title: Intel Weighs GlobalFoundries Buy to Bolster Capacity
feed.summary: Intel is reportedly considering an acquisition of GlobalFoundries for as much as $30 billion to quickly bolster its manufacturing capacity. This move aligns with CEO Pat Gelsinger's strategy to revive Intel's foundry operations and compete with industry leaders like TSMC, following past manufacturing setbacks. While GlobalFoundries no longer focuses on leading-edge silicon, an acquisition would provide Intel with immediate capacity and potentially impact smaller semiconductor customers.

feed.title: High volatility in Verb Technology Company Inc. stock price on Friday which ended trading at $2.92
feed.summary: Verb Technology Company Inc. (VERB) stock experienced high volatility, falling -8.75% on July 16, 2021, to $2.92, despite a 52.88% rise over the preceding two weeks. The stock shows short and long-term buy signals from moving averages and a strong rising trend, leading to a "Buy" recommendation from StockInvest.us. However, a sell signal was issued from a recent pivot top, and the stock is considered "very high risk" due to its volatility and wide prediction interval.

feed.title: Best Buy Is Selling Nvidia RTX 30 Series GPUs At MSRP Tomorrow
feed.summary: Best Buy stores nationwide will be selling Nvidia RTX 30 series graphics cards at their original MSRP starting tomorrow, July 20th. Due to severe shortages and price inflation, this offers a rare opportunity for customers to purchase these GPUs at normal prices. Best Buy will be implementing a ticketing system at participating stores to manage demand and discourage scalping.

feed.title: Newcomer Viridi Funds Launches Clean Energy Crypto Mining ETF, 'RIGZ'
feed.summary: Viridi Funds has launched its first ETF, the Viridi Cleaner Energy Crypto-Mining & Semiconductor ETF (RIGZ), offering investors exposure to environmentally sustainable cryptocurrency. The actively managed fund will invest in companies across the crypto mining industry and infrastructure, including semiconductor producers, mining rig manufacturers, and crypto mining companies. RIGZ aims to align purpose and profit by focusing on sustainable practices within the growing crypto ecosystem, particularly in North America where over 50% of Bitcoin mining uses renewable energy sources.

feed.title: ON Semi’s Image Sensors and SiPMs Give AutoX a Leg Up on Autonomous Driving
feed.summary: AutoX, a leader in level-4 autonomous vehicle technology, has secured a license to operate RoboTaxis in China, leveraging ON Semiconductor's image sensors and SiPM technology for 360° vision. The integration includes twenty-eight 8.3 Megapixel image sensors and four LiDAR sensors, significantly enhancing safety and eliminating blind spots in dense urban environments. This partnership highlights the critical role of advanced sensing technology in the advancement of autonomous driving, despite legal and financial hurdles faced by other companies aiming for higher levels of automation.

feed.title: Aehr Receives $10.8 Million Order for Production Test and Burn-in of Silicon Carbide Power Semiconductors for Electric Vehicles
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems announced a $10.8 million order from a Fortune 500 semiconductor supplier for FOX-XP™ systems and WaferPak™ Contactors. These systems are crucial for testing and burn-in of silicon carbide power semiconductors used in electric vehicles, addressing high infant mortality rates and ensuring reliability. The order reflects the growing demand in the electric vehicle market, with shipments expected within six months.

feed.title: Chip design giant Cadence launches AI platform to speed processor development
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems Inc. has launched Cerebrus Intelligent Chip Explorer, an AI platform designed to accelerate processor development and enhance the productivity of semiconductor engineers. The platform utilizes machine learning, specifically reinforcement learning, to automate and optimize various stages of chip design, including register-transfer level development and floorplan creation. Early adopters like Renesas Electronics Corp. and Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd.'s foundry business have already seen significant improvements in chip performance.

feed.title: Intel CEO Says Chip Shortage Could Stretch Into 2023
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger believes the global semiconductor shortage could extend into 2023, joining a growing consensus that supply chain disruptions will persist. The shortage is impacting various industries, leading to rising prices and production delays for goods ranging from consumer gadgets to automobiles. While some easing is expected later this year, a full return to supply-and-demand balance in the semiconductor industry could take one to two years due to the time required to build manufacturing capacity.

feed.title: Cyberpower Hyper Liquid Ultra RTX PC
feed.summary: This article reviews Cyberpower's high-end Hyper Liquid Ultra RTX PC, priced at £4,899. It highlights the system's powerful components, including a watercooled AMD Ryzen 9 5950X and Nvidia GeForce RTX 3080 Ti, and critically assesses its build and performance. While praising the component selection generally, the review points out areas for improvement such as the motherboard choice, idle noise levels, and RGB control.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries' second fab sure to bring more jobs, tech prestige to Capital Region
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries plans to build a second chip manufacturing facility, Fab 8.2, in Saratoga County, which is expected to cost over $10 billion and create 1,000 new jobs. This expansion is contingent on federal funding from the U.S. Innovation and Competition Act, which aims to boost U.S. semiconductor manufacturing amidst a global chip shortage. The project, along with an existing Fab 8 expansion, will significantly increase chip output and solidify the Capital Region's role in the global tech industry.

feed.title: The Ångstrom era: Intel unveils an ambitious semiconductor roadmap
feed.summary: Intel has announced an ambitious new semiconductor roadmap, moving away from nanometer-based naming conventions to a new system that includes "Intel 7," "Intel 4," and "Intel 3." The company aims to reclaim market leadership by 2025 and will enter the "Ångstrom era" with its Intel 20A chips, featuring new RibbonFET architecture and PowerVia technology. This strategy seeks to address past manufacturing delays and compete more effectively with rivals like TSMC and Samsung despite facing a challenging market.

feed.title: Intel to build Qualcomm chips, aims to catch foundry rivals by 2025
feed.summary: Intel announced its factories will begin building chips for Qualcomm and Amazon, aiming to regain its leadership in chip manufacturing by 2025 against rivals like TSMC and Samsung. The company outlined a new roadmap with five chipmaking technologies and a revised naming scheme to reflect its competitive standing. This move signifies Intel's commitment to overcoming past delays and establishing itself as a leading foundry.

feed.title: Hey Intel, AMD’s CEO is also ready to ‘fight for every socket,’ while producing strong growth
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su echoed Intel's pledge to "fight for every socket" in the crucial data-center market, demonstrating confidence as AMD continues strong growth. AMD is outperforming Intel by increasing its annual guidance and seeing data-center sales top 20% of its revenue.

feed.title: Hey Intel, AMD’s CEO is also ready to ‘fight for every socket,’ while producing strong growth
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su responded to Intel's promise to "fight for every socket" by stating AMD is also ready for the competition. This comes as AMD demonstrates strong growth, particularly with data-center sales exceeding 20% of its revenue, leading to an increase in its annual guidance. Su's comments highlight the escalating rivalry in the chip market, particularly in the crucial data-center segment.

feed.title: Corsair One a200 gaming PC review
feed.summary: The Corsair One a200 is a compact gaming PC that combines an AMD Ryzen 9 5900X CPU and an Nvidia GeForce RTX 3080 GPU in a small form factor, delivering excellent 4K gaming performance. Despite its size, it manages impressive thermal performance for the GPU due to a dual-liquid cooling system, although the CPU runs a bit hot. The PC is praised for its powerful components, ease of setup, and quiet operation, though its upgradeability is limited compared to larger systems.

feed.title: Intel vs. AMD: Which Semiconductor Stock Is a Better Pick?
feed.summary: The semiconductor market is projected to grow significantly, prompting a comparison between industry giants Intel and AMD. While Intel is undergoing a business transformation and aims for process leadership by 2025, analysts remain skeptical due to past execution issues. AMD, however, posted stellar Q2 results driven by strong demand in data center and gaming, leading analysts to rate it as a Strong Buy with higher upside potential compared to Intel.

feed.title: Mouser Offers a Wide Variety of Microchip Products
feed.summary: Mouser Electronics, an authorized global distributor for Microchip Technology, offers a vast selection of Microchip products, including over 22,400 parts and 1,342 development kits. The article highlights popular Microchip solutions such as their ultra-low-power LoRa® solutions, the PolarFire SoC FPGA Icicle Kit for Linux-ready designs, and the AVR128DB48 Curiosity Nano Evaluation Kit. Mouser also collaborates with Microchip to provide informative content on advanced electronics design.

feed.title: OpenAI debuts new AI programming language for creating neural networks
feed.summary: OpenAI has released Triton 1.0, a new programming language designed to simplify and accelerate the creation of high-speed machine learning algorithms, especially those running on Nvidia GPUs. Triton automates complex code optimizations previously requiring extensive CUDA experience, thereby speeding up AI projects and enabling more efficient algorithm development. It optimizes data movement between GPU memory, data distribution across SRAM circuits, and calculation distribution across CUDA cores.

feed.title: AMD Announces Radeon RX 6600 XT GPU
feed.summary: AMD has formally unveiled its new Radeon RX 6600 XT graphics card, set to launch in mid-August with a starting price of $379. This GPU is designed primarily for PC gamers using older hardware and aims to provide a substantial upgrade for 1080p gaming, although it's not suited for 4K. Despite its target market, the ongoing GPU shortage raises concerns about potential scalping and bots impacting availability upon release.

feed.title: CEO interview: Globalfoundries’ Tom Caulfield on the European project
feed.summary: Globalfoundries CEO Tom Caulfield discusses Europe's ambitious goal to double its chip manufacturing capacity by 2030, emphasizing the need for government partnership and investment. He highlights Europe's strengths in lithography (ASML) and FDSOI technology, cautioning against an overemphasis on leading-edge 2nm process manufacturing, and advocating for a focus on established nodes crucial for European industries like automotive. Caulfield also confirms Globalfoundries' commitment to expanding its Dresden fab with government support.

feed.title: The U.K. may block Nvidia’s acquisition of Arm over security risks
feed.summary: The U.K. government is considering blocking Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm Ltd. due to potential national security concerns. The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) delivered an assessment with worrying implications, leading the U.K. to be inclined to reject the takeover or impose strict conditions. Arm's critical role in the semiconductor industry and Nvidia's status as a direct competitor to many of Arm's customers are central to the concerns, prompting Nvidia to state its commitment to addressing any issues the U.K. government may have.

feed.title: Samsung, TSMC in heated race for industry’s smallest 3 nm process node
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics and TSMC are in an intense competition to lead the semiconductor industry with the 3-nanometer process node technology, aiming for mass production in 2022. This rivalry is further fueled by Intel's entry into the foundry business, pledging significant investments and aiming to become a top player by 2025, challenging both Samsung and TSMC in advanced chip manufacturing and market share. The race is also extending to 2-nanometer technology, pushing these companies to invest heavily in expanding facilities and R&D.

feed.title: Venkat Thanvantri talks about Cadence’s Cerebrus Revolutionizing Chip Design
feed.summary: Venkat Thanvantri, VP of R&D, AI/ML Digital and Signoff at Cadence, discusses how Cadence's Cerebrus Intelligent Chip Explorer is revolutionizing chip design. The tool uses machine learning and distributed computing to improve power, performance, and area (PPA) more quickly, addressing the growing complexity of SoC designs. He emphasizes that AI/ML helps automate parts of the design process to speed time to market, distinct from the chip shortage which is a manufacturing and supply-demand issue.

feed.title: AMD Trying To Increase Worldwide Semiconductor Availability
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is making significant investments across its supply chain to increase worldwide semiconductor availability amidst a global chip shortage. The company is responding to the surge in demand for PCs, re-energized by remote work and learning during the pandemic, and is working to bridge the gap in semiconductor supply which is expected to last until at least 2023. AMD's Corporate Vice President, David McAfee, highlighted their efforts to source additional capacity and noted the challenge of the limited number of specialized chip fabrication facilities globally.

feed.title: Microchip Technology sees record sales as computer chip supply issues persist
feed.summary: Microchip Technology (MCHP) reported record-setting sales at the beginning of its 2022 fiscal year, driven by the ongoing global demand for computer chips. CEO Ganesh Moorthy indicated that the entire semiconductor supply chain remains constrained, suggesting that chip shortages will extend into 2022. The company, based in Chandler, is experiencing strong demand amidst an industry-wide supply crunch.

feed.title: NVIDIA maintains dominant position in 2020 market for AI processors for cloud and data center
feed.summary: NVIDIA maintained its dominant position in the 2020 global market for AI processors used in cloud and data centers, holding an 80.6% share of global revenue. The company generated $3.2 billion in cloud and data center AI processor revenue, up from $1.8 billion in 2019, primarily due to its leadership in GPU-derived chips. Omdia predicts that alternative suppliers will gain significant market share in the coming years despite NVIDIA's current advantage.

feed.title: Colorful Releases Special Edition iGame RTX 3070 LHR For Bilibili E-Sports Team
feed.summary: Colorful has released a special edition iGame RTX 3070 LHR graphics card exclusively for China's Bilibili E-Sports team and market. This unique card features a 12-pin power connection and a dual-fan design similar to RTX 3080 and RTX 3090 Founders Edition cards. It offers a slight overclocking boost and a low hash rate, tailored for the e-sports segment in specified regions.

feed.title: All AI Do Is Win: NVIDIA Research Nabs ‘Best in Show’ With Digital Avatars at SIGGRAPH
feed.summary: NVIDIA Research received the ‘Best in Show’ award at SIGGRAPH 2021 for their digital avatar technology, which integrates four AI models. This technology allows for the creation of lifelike virtual characters from a single photo, natural 3D facial animation, and text-to-speech conversion. The innovative tools aim to simplify the creation of digital avatars for various applications, including videoconferencing and storytelling.

feed.title: In Depth: Chinese Chipmaking Upstarts Race to Rival Nvidia
feed.summary: Chinese venture capital is actively supporting the development of next-generation microprocessors, as numerous startups emerge to challenge Nvidia's dominance in the GPGPU market. These companies are attracting significant investment and recruiting top talent, aiming to capitalize on China's push for semiconductor self-reliance and the growing demand for AI computing chips. Despite the enthusiasm and funding, experts caution that success will require billions of dollars to build comparable software ecosystems and overcome challenges like intellectual property and relationships with established tech giants.

feed.title: BIOSTAR LAUNCHES AMD RADEON™ RX 6600 XT GRAPHICS CARD
feed.summary: BIOSTAR has announced the availability of its new AMD Radeon RX 6600 XT graphics card, designed for high-performance 1080p gaming. This card features AMD RDNA 2 architecture, 32 compute units, 8GB GDDR6 memory, and supports AMD Smart Access Memory, DirectX 12 Ultimate, and AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution for immersive gaming experiences. It boasts game clock speeds up to 2359 Mhz and boost clock speeds up to 2589 Mhz, along with custom axial fans for efficient cooling.

feed.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang will receive the chip industry's top award
feed.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is being recognized with the Semiconductor Industry Association's prestigious Robert Noyce Award. The award acknowledges Nvidia's significant contributions across gaming, self-driving cars, and scientific computing. Huang will receive the honor at a dinner ceremony in November.

feed.title: Samsung Has Its Own AI-Designed Chip. Soon, Others Will Too
feed.summary: Samsung is utilizing AI-designed chips developed with Synopsys's new software, DSO.ai, for its Exynos processors, marking a significant step in the semiconductor industry. This innovative approach, also being explored by companies like Google, Nvidia, and IBM, leverages reinforcement learning to automate and accelerate the complex chip design process, potentially leading to novel designs and improved performance. While AI-driven design is currently expensive, experts anticipate it will become a standard tool in the future.

feed.title: 5 reasons why AMD stock could rally more than 20% from here, according to Bank of America analyst
feed.summary: A Bank of America analyst, Vivek Arya, believes AMD stock could rally more than 20% due to several factors, including the company's potential to expand gross margins and double earnings per share within the next two to three years. This projection comes after AMD shares have already seen a significant surge of over 50% in the last three months, and the stock is considered undervalued compared to other tech companies with similar growth.

feed.title: Rahul Singh
feed.summary: This page provides a profile of Rahul Singh, Chief Technology Officer at Ford Pro. It details his role in leading Ford Pro’s technology and software platforms to create value for commercial customers. The profile also covers his past roles at Ford Motor Company, ATi Graphics, and Xtreme Labs, along with his educational background.

feed.title: The Top 10 Women in the Data Centre Industry
feed.summary: Data Centre Magazine highlights ten influential women who are leading the way in the data center industry, addressing the underrepresentation of women in senior and technical roles. The article features profiles of these leaders, detailing their roles, experience, and contributions to the field. This celebration aims to inspire and emphasize the importance of diversity and inclusion.

feed.title: Samsung Is Using AI to Design Its Next Exynos Chipset for Smartphones
feed.summary: Samsung is reportedly leveraging artificial intelligence, specifically Synopsys' DSO.ai tool, to design its next Exynos chipsets for smartphones. This AI-driven approach aims to automate and accelerate the complex chip development process, potentially improving performance and power efficiency. While Samsung has not confirmed which future products, such as the rumored Exynos 2200, will utilize these AI-designed chips, industry experts suggest AI is well-suited for arranging billions of transistors on a chip.

feed.title: Nvidia earnings will be a data-center show, but don’t miss the software on the side stage
feed.summary: Nvidia's upcoming second-quarter earnings report is expected to heavily feature data-center sales, with analysts predicting a significant rise in server-chip sales. However, the article highlights the increasing importance of Nvidia's software in its overall valuation, suggesting it should be compared more with software companies. While hardware is key, the software supporting Nvidia's graphics processing chips is a critical, often overlooked, aspect of its market position.

feed.title: Navitas Semicondcutor Is Going Public via a SPAC
feed.summary: Navitas Semiconductor, a company focused on gallium nitride (GaN) power chips, is set to go public through a merger with a Special Purpose Acquisition Company (SPAC). Its CEO believes in a bright future for these chips, which aim to transform the semiconductor industry. This move marks Navitas as the latest company to utilize the SPAC route for its public listing.

feed.title: Mynet delivers exceptional mobile gaming experience with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, cuts costs by 80%
feed.summary: Mynet, a Japan-based online business company, has significantly improved its mobile gaming operations and cut costs by 80% by migrating its game titles to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). The company, which specializes in acquiring and repackaging existing game titles, leveraged OCI's compute, storage, networking, and other cloud services to achieve high availability, performance, and scalability. This transition also enabled Mynet to implement a multicloud strategy and achieve smart operations through big data, extending the longevity and profitability of its game titles.

feed.title: Rambus Innovates 8.4 Gbps HBM3-ready Memory Subsystem
feed.summary: Rambus Inc. has announced its HBM3-ready memory interface subsystem, featuring a fully-integrated PHY and digital controller that supports data rates up to 8.4 Gbps, delivering over 1 terabyte per second of bandwidth. This solution aims to meet the demanding memory bandwidth requirements of AI/ML training and high-performance computing (HPC) applications, building on Rambus's expertise in high-speed signaling and 2.5D memory system architecture. The company also provides interposer and package reference designs to accelerate customers' product development.

feed.title: Rambus Outs HBM3 Details: 1.075 TBps of Bandwidth, 16 Channels, 16-Hi Stacks
feed.summary: Rambus has unveiled new details about its HBM3-ready combined PHY and memory controller, revealing specifications that promise a significant leap in memory performance. The new HBM3 standard could achieve up to 8.4 Gbps-per-pin, 1.075 TB/s of throughput, and support for 16 channels and 16-Hi memory stacks, more than doubling the capacity and bandwidth of HBM2E. These advancements are expected to radically speed up future GPUs and SoCs, with the first chips leveraging this design anticipated in late 2022 or early 2023.

feed.title: AMD Set To Become TSMC’s 2nd Largest Customer – Apple Car Chip Production Rumored For China
feed.summary: AMD is projected to become TSMC's second-largest customer, behind Apple, due to strong growth across its chip segments, especially EPYC server products. This increased demand from AMD, Apple, and MediaTek is accelerating order flows for TSMC's 3nm process. Additionally, there are rumors that automotive chip production for an "Apple Car" might take place at TSMC's Nanjing facility using older 28nm process nodes.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries files for IPO, dashing hopes of Intel acquisition
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries has filed for an initial public offering, reportedly ending Intel's hopes of acquiring the company for as much as $30 billion. The chipmaker, owned by Abu Dhabi's Mubdala Investment Co., plans to reveal its IPO filing in October, aiming to go public by year-end or early next year. The decision is influenced by concerns that a sale to Intel would alienate key customers like AMD and face antitrust scrutiny, all while a global semiconductor shortage persists.

feed.title: Tesla unveils chip to train A.I. models inside its data centers
feed.summary: Tesla has unveiled a custom D1 chip designed for training artificial-intelligence networks in its data centers, which is part of its Dojo supercomputer system. An employee stated it will be the fastest AI-training computer, aiming to process extensive computing work for models that recognize items from car camera feeds. Tesla also continues to offer and develop its "Full Self-Driving Capability" for vehicles, despite criticism regarding its level of autonomy and recent investigations.

feed.title: Toyota cuts production amid chip shortage, COVID
feed.summary: Toyota announced a significant cut in its global production for September, reducing output by 40% from 900,000 to approximately 500,000 vehicles. This decision is primarily due to the ongoing global semiconductor chip shortage, exacerbated by increased demand for consumer electronics during the pandemic, and other COVID-related supply chain disruptions. The production cuts will affect models like the Corolla, Prius, and Land Cruiser, and will impact manufacturing operations in Japan, North America, China, and Europe, causing Toyota's shares to fall.

feed.title: Intel CEO Calls Chip Maker ‘Willing Buyer’ as Semiconductor Industry Consolidates
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has stated that the company is a "willing buyer" as the semiconductor industry consolidates. He plans to use mergers and acquisitions to support Intel's revival strategy. Gelsinger expects consolidation to continue in the industry and sees Intel as a key consolidator.

feed.title: Niche chip maker SkyWater plays key role in U.S. supply strategy
feed.summary: SkyWater Technology Inc., a niche U.S. chip manufacturer, is playing a crucial role in the Biden administration's strategy to bolster domestic semiconductor supply chains and reduce reliance on foreign production, particularly from Taiwan. Despite its smaller size compared to industry giants, SkyWater's status as the only U.S. investor-owned fab and its advanced 3-D chip stacking technology, backed by significant military funding, position it as a key player in the intensifying U.S.-China competition for technological supremacy. The article highlights the global push by various nations, including the U.S., Europe, and Japan, to strengthen their domestic chip industries amidst supply chain disruptions and geopolitical concerns, though some experts question the efficacy of government interventions.

feed.title: Synopsys design software uses AI to make chips 26% more efficient
feed.summary: Synopsys Inc. announced that its AI software, DSO.ai, helped a customer achieve a 26% gain in the power efficiency of a computer chip, a significant improvement usually associated with new manufacturing technology. This AI system integrates with Synopsys's chip design suite to optimize speed, power efficiency, and cost, with companies like Samsung and Renesas already utilizing it. The latest advancement allows the AI to consider the software running on a chip for further optimization.

feed.title: Intel Lands Pentagon Deal to Support Domestic Chip Making
feed.summary: Intel Corp. has secured a deal with the Pentagon to provide commercial foundry services as part of the Rapid Assured Microelectronics Prototypes - Commercial (RAMP-C) program. This initiative aims to bolster domestic design and production of advanced microchips, addressing a global chip shortage and supporting the U.S. government's long-term national security needs. Intel will join other companies like IBM and Synopsys in this effort, with plans to invest $20 billion in new factories in Arizona.
feed.banner_image: Photo: Edwin Cheng for The Wall Street Journal

feed.title: Worldwide PC Demand to Continue Growing Throughout 2021, IDC Notes
feed.summary: According to a report from the International Data Corporation (IDC), worldwide PC shipments are projected to grow by 14% in 2021, reaching 347 million units. This continued growth is primarily driven by the strong demand for laptop PCs, despite short-term supply chain constraints. Although demand is expected to slow down by 2025 as consumers redirect spending towards other activities, the overall PC market will remain significantly larger than pre-pandemic levels.

feed.title: TSMC May Charter its Own Boxship to Transport Chip-Making Equipment
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is reportedly considering chartering its own container ship to transport manufacturing equipment to its new facility in Phoenix, Arizona. This strategic move aims to significantly reduce the high costs associated with conventional containerized freight given current market rates. The first plant, costing $12 billion, is expected to begin mass production in 2024, with the first batch of containers anticipated to depart in October.

feed.title: A massive restock of Nvidia RTX 30-series GPUs is happening at Best Buy today
feed.summary: Best Buy stores across the US are holding a major in-store restock of Nvidia RTX 30-series graphics cards on August 26, 2021. The restock will include highly sought-after GPUs like the RTX 3080 Ti, RTX 3080, RTX 3070, RTX 3070 Ti, RTX 3060 Ti, and RTX 3090, with approximately 17,000 units available in total. Customers hoping to purchase a card should arrive early, as tickets guaranteeing a GPU will be distributed starting at 7:30 AM local time, and items are limited to one per customer.

feed.title: Western Digital reportedly in advanced talks to merge with Kioxia
feed.summary: Western Digital is reportedly in advanced talks to merge with Kioxia Holdings Corp in a deal valued at over $20 billion, which would significantly consolidate the flash storage industry. This potential acquisition would create a giant comparable in size to Samsung in the semiconductor market. The two companies already have a long-standing partnership, including joint ventures in 3D NAND manufacturing.

feed.title: Synopsys' AI-Assisted EDA Tools Aim To Cut GPU Design Costs in Half
feed.summary: Synopsys' new AI-assisted Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools promise to significantly reduce the cost and time required for chip design, particularly for GPUs. The DSO.ai platform can optimize various aspects of chip design, potentially cutting development time from 24 months to 24 weeks and reducing costs by as much as 50%. Companies like Samsung, Google, and Nvidia are already adopting this technology, which could also democratize chip development for smaller firms.

feed.title: NVIDIA Stock Price and Forecast: Why is NVDA stock going up?
feed.summary: NVIDIA stock continues to rise, hitting new all-time highs despite broader market nervousness ahead of the Jackson Hole symposium. A Reuters report about the US Department of Energy purchasing a supercomputer with Nvidia chips is cited as a catalyst. The article also touches on concerns from the UK's Competition and Markets Authority regarding Nvidia's proposed acquisition of ARM.

feed.title: The Chip Has Hit The Fan
feed.summary: TSMC is delaying its 3-nanometer chip process until the second half of 2022 and plans to increase prices for both advanced (7nm, 5nm) and older chip processes (55nm, 40nm, 28nm, 16nm). These changes are influenced by the complexity of advanced chip manufacturing, supply chain issues exacerbated by the pandemic, and high demand. The article also discusses potential challenges from competitors like Intel and Samsung and the broader geopolitical implications of chip manufacturing dependence.

feed.title: Semiconductor ETF
feed.summary: The VanEck Semiconductor UCITS ETF provides targeted exposure to the semiconductor sector, crucial for the ongoing "fourth industrial revolution" encompassing AI, 5G, and IoT. This ETF, the first of its kind in Europe, invests in pure-play semiconductor companies globally, focusing on those generating at least half their revenue from semiconductors. It highlights the high barriers to entry in the semiconductor industry, which protect profit margins for leading manufacturers.

feed.title: Down to a Science: How Johnson & Johnson Boosts Its Business With MLOps
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson is actively integrating data science and Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) into its business to enhance manufacturing, clinical trial enrollment, and forecasting. The company emphasizes "decision science" to derive actionable insights from models and has established a Data Science Council and "bilingual data scientists" to bridge domain expertise with data science skills. This strategy, supported by powerful AI infrastructure from platforms like NVIDIA GPU-accelerated Domino Data Lab, has already demonstrated significant impact, such as improving forecasting accuracy in its vision care business and boosting revenue.

feed.title: Look for PCIe 5.0, NVMe 2.0 to boost storage performance
feed.summary: Enterprises requiring high-performance storage for AI and ML workloads should anticipate new products utilizing PCIe 5.0 and NVMe 2.0 specifications. PCIe 5.0 doubles data rates, and NVMe 2.0 introduces command sets for key-value (KV) and Zoned Namespace (ZNS) technologies, though product availability will lag, with software changes needed to fully leverage NVMe 2.0's benefits.

feed.title: AMD’s Patents Signalling a Deeper Commitment to Quantum?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has applied for two advanced quantum computing patents, indicating a serious commitment to the field. These patents, "Look-ahead Teleportation for Reliable Computation in Multi-SIMD Quantum Processor" and "Variation-Aware Qubit Movement Scheme for Noise Intermediate Scale Quantum Era Computers," showcase AMD's deep understanding and expertise in developing actual quantum solutions, not just theoretical research. While only two patents, their sophisticated nature suggests a significant step by AMD into quantum technology.

feed.title: Corsair partners with Ubisoft for Far Cry 6 immersive experience, AMD-powered PC giveaway
feed.summary: Corsair is collaborating with Ubisoft for Far Cry 6, integrating its iCUE RGB lighting system directly into the game to create an immersive experience. The company is also offering copies of Far Cry 6 with qualifying iCUE product purchases and running a sweepstakes for a custom, AMD-powered Far Cry 6-themed PC. This partnership extends Corsair's previous successful integrations with Ubisoft titles, enhancing in-game actions with synchronized lighting effects.

feed.title: A Brief History of the Single-Chip DSP, Part II
feed.summary: This article chronicles the shift from single-chip DSPs to FPGAs as the dominant technology for digital signal processing, particularly highlighting the competitive era of the 1980s and 1990s. It details how FPGAs, by incorporating numerous hardware multiplier/accumulators, surpassed traditional DSPs in performance, leading to the latter's decline in high-performance applications. The discussion also touches on the convergence of CPUs and DSPs and the rise of Application-Specific Standard Products (ASSPs).

feed.title: Applied Materials improves die-to-wafer and wafer-to-wafer bonding
feed.summary: Applied Materials has announced advancements in die-to-wafer and wafer-to-wafer bonding technologies to improve heterogeneous chip design and integration. This includes enhanced software modeling and simulation, a partnership with BE Semiconductor Industries (Besi) for die-based hybrid bonding equipment, and a joint development agreement with EV Group (EVG) focusing on wafer bonding and related processes. Additionally, Applied Materials' acquisition of Tango Systems has yielded benefits for panel-level processing.

feed.title: Toyota Motor cuts 2021-22 vehicle output on chip shortage, COVID-19
feed.summary: Toyota Motor Corp has reduced its full-year production outlook for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2022, by 300,000 vehicles, from 9.30 million to 9 million, due to the global semiconductor chip shortage and increased COVID-19 cases in Southeast Asia. This reduction will result in 400,000 fewer vehicles produced in September and October, impacting automotive steel demand and leading to further closures of manufacturing lines in Japan. Toyota remains uncertain about production beyond November but notes that current demand for its vehicles is very strong.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics Partners with Xilinx on Rad-Hard FPGAs Using ST Space-Qualified Regulators
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has partnered with Xilinx to develop a power solution for the Xilinx Kintex UltraScale XQRKU060 radiation-tolerant FPGA for space applications. This solution utilizes ST’s QML-V qualified voltage regulators, including the RHRPMPOL01 and RHFL6000A, which offer high fixed-point accuracy and strong resistance to radiation effects. The collaboration aims to simplify and accelerate the development of flexible, reprogrammable space systems, leveraging the FPGA's ability to be reconfigured in orbit.

feed.title: HPE finds second new CTO in 16 months with Fidelma Russo
feed.summary: HPE has appointed Fidelma Russo, formerly of VMware, as its new Chief Technology Officer, marking the second CTO change in 16 months. Russo's extensive background in cloud services, software, and infrastructure is expected to drive innovation for HPE's GreenLake edge-to-cloud platform. This move signals HPE's strategic shift towards cloud-centric solutions to compete with off-premises rivals, despite strong Q3 financials attributed partly to increased hardware prices.

feed.title: Jensen Huang: The 100 Most Influential People of 2021
feed.summary: Jensen Huang, CEO of Nvidia, has been named one of TIME's 100 Most Influential People of 2021. He is recognized for his pivotal role in advancing artificial intelligence through the development of Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), which transformed computing capabilities for complex AI tasks. His technical prowess, compassionate leadership, and support for education have positioned Nvidia to continue driving technological advancements in the AI era.

feed.title: Manufacturing the Future of AI with Edge Computing | NVIDIA Technical Blog
feed.summary: This article discusses how edge computing is transforming the manufacturing industry by integrating IoT sensors and AI to improve operational efficiency and productivity. It highlights the benefits of edge AI, such as ultra-low latency processing, enhanced security, and bandwidth savings, and explores various use cases including predictive maintenance, quality control, equipment effectiveness, yield optimization, factory floor optimization, supply chain analytics, and worker safety. The author emphasizes that edge computing enables real-time decision-making on the factory floor, minimizing downtime and maximizing output.

feed.title: Caixin Explains: What’s at Stake in the Massive Unigroup Restructuring
feed.summary: Tsinghua Unigroup Co. Ltd., a Chinese semiconductor giant, is undergoing a massive bankruptcy restructuring to address its significant debts and revive its promising businesses. Several companies, including Alibaba Group and various government-backed enterprises, have expressed interest in bidding for Unigroup's assets, which include its critical chipmaking and cloud computing units. The restructuring aims to find strategic investors with the financial capacity to take over and further invest in key projects like Yangtze Memory Technologies, crucial for China's semiconductor independence.

feed.title: Shares of this obscure chip component supplier have surged by over 1200% in the past three years
feed.summary: Shares of Nan Ya Printed Circuit Board Corp., an obscure Taiwanese company that supplies essential Ajinomoto build-up film (ABF) substrates for chipmaking, have surged over 1200% in the past three years. This surge is due to ABF substrate becoming a critical bottleneck in the semiconductor industry, with supply expected to remain constrained until at least 2025. Major tech companies are struggling to secure enough of this component, leading to increased profits and investment for substrate manufacturers.

feed.title: Home Assistant Yellow – A Raspberry Pi CM4 based automation gateway (Crowdfunding)
feed.summary: Home Assistant has launched its second hardware product, the Home Assistant Yellow automation gateway, which is based on the Raspberry Pi CM4 module. This new device supports Zigbee, OpenThread, and will soon support Matter, offering broad compatibility with over 1,000 smart home devices and services. The Home Assistant Yellow is available for crowdfunding on Crowd Supply, with various kit options starting at $99.

feed.title: Samsung to make Tesla’s HW 4.0 self-driving auto chip
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics Co. has won the contract to manufacture Tesla Inc.'s next-generation hardware 4 (HW 4.0) chip for its autonomous driving technology, beating out TSMC. Samsung plans to mass-produce the HW 4.0 chip using 7-nanometer processing technology, ensuring higher production yields and stable functions for Tesla's upcoming vehicles like the Cybertruck. This partnership strengthens Samsung's position in the global foundry market as it competes with TSMC and expands its collaborations with other tech giants like Google and Amazon.

feed.title: Cover Story: The rise of test equipment giants amid tech boom
feed.summary: The Malaysian semiconductor industry, historically dominated by OSAT companies, is seeing a significant rise in automated test equipment (ATE) manufacturers due to the Fourth Industrial Revolution and AI. These ATE makers, such as ViTrox, Greatech, and Pentamaster, have outperformed OSAT companies in share price and are now comparable in market capitalization, attracting a premium from investors. The article explores the opportunities and challenges for ATE manufacturers, including views on MNC presence, diversification strategies, and the potential for Malaysia to become a regional hub for advanced technology equipment.

feed.title: Intel breaks ground on $20 bln Arizona plants as U.S. chip factory race heats up
feed.summary: Intel has broken ground on two new $20 billion chip factories in Arizona, named Fab 52 and Fab 62, as part of its strategy to regain leadership in advanced chip manufacturing and become a major producer for external customers. These plants will be crucial for Intel's goal to lead in chip technology by 2025 and will bolster the U.S. chip supply chain. CEO Pat Gelsinger noted the facilities will also serve outside companies like Qualcomm and Amazon, and Intel plans a new U.S. campus with additional factories by year-end.
